iNavFormulaDesc: NAV × (1 + index return) × (1 + FX change) = iNAV
iNavTooltipDesc: Indicative NAV is the real-time estimated net asset value, calculated by applying index and FX changes to the previous day's NAV.
iNavDisclaimer: This value is a real-time estimated price calculated based on previous day's NAV, underlying ETF returns, and FX changes.

guide.title: ETF investing basics
guide.subtitle: NAV, iNAV, premium/discount, tracking error
guide.description: Everyone picks ETFs differently, but a few concepts are worth knowing before you invest. Here we explain NAV, iNAV, premium (discount), and tracking error.
guide.sectionNavInavIntro: Two measures of an ETF’s value.
guide.navDefinition: The ETF’s net asset value calculated after the market closes each day, using the value and quantity of the fund’s holdings. NAV is (total assets minus liabilities, e.g. management fees) divided by the number of shares outstanding.
guide.inavDefinition: ETFs trade in real time like stocks, so the market needs an estimate of the fund’s value during the session. The exchange publishes this real-time estimate, called iNAV. It is an “indicative” value because it cannot be exact.
guide.navInavSummary1: NAV: ETF’s net asset value calculated after the close
guide.navInavSummary2: iNAV: Real-time estimated NAV provided during the session
guide.sectionPremiumTrackingIntro: How price differs from value, and how well the ETF tracks its index.
guide.premiumDefinition: The market price of an ETF can differ from its NAV (or iNAV). That difference is the premium or discount; the ratio is often called the premium (괴리율). When market price > NAV the ETF trades at a premium; when market price < NAV it trades at a discount.
guide.trackingErrorDefinition: Premium measures the gap between market price and NAV. Tracking error measures how closely the ETF’s NAV follows its benchmark index. Lower tracking error means the fund tracks the index better. It is affected by replication method, fees, trading costs, dividends, and interest.
guide.premiumTrackingSummary1: Premium: the ratio of the difference between market price and NAV (iNAV)
guide.premiumTrackingSummary2: Tracking error: the difference between the index and the ETF’s NAV over time
guide.sectionServiceUsageIntro: This platform computes premium in real time and shows reference buy/sell signals.
guide.serviceUsagePremium: Premium: we compute the premium of the current market price vs. iNAV, where iNAV is estimated from prior NAV and index and FX moves.
guide.serviceUsageSignal: Buy/sell/neutral signals: we show reference signals based on premium bands you set (e.g. buy when premium ≤ −1%, sell when ≥ +1%).
guide.serviceUsageDisclaimer: iNAV and premium are estimates and may differ from official NAV and market conditions. Investment decisions are your own responsibility.
guide.referenceSamsungGuide: Samsung Fund KODEX ETF basics (NAV, iNAV, premium, tracking error)

about.title: About this service
about.description: A free analysis tool for Korea-listed US ETFs: premium/NAV analysis, buy/sell signals, premium trend charts, and strategy simulation.
about.sectionWhatContent: This platform analyzes real-time premium (discount/premium to NAV) for US index-tracking ETFs listed on the Korean exchange (e.g. TIGER US Nasdaq 100, KODEX US S&P 500, ACE US Semiconductor). It provides buy/sell/neutral signals based on iNAV (indicative NAV) and uses market data from NAVER Finance. You can also view premium history charts, run strategy simulations, and compare ETFs that track the same index.
about.featurePremium: Real-time premium analysis and iNAV-based fair value estimate
about.featureTrend: Premium history chart with cheap/expensive zone interpretation
about.featureCompare: Compare NAV, iNAV, and premium across same-index ETFs
about.sectionTargetContent: Useful for investors trading US index ETFs, or anyone looking to time entries by premium level or compare same-index ETFs. Information is for reference only; investment decisions are your own responsibility.
about.sectionDisclaimerContent: Figures and signals on this service are for reference only and do not constitute investment advice or predictions. iNAV and premium are estimates based on prior NAV, index, and FX data and may differ from official NAV and market conditions. All investment decisions are your own responsibility.
